Meaning: Days since last non-zero sale
How to build this feature?: For each store_id + item_id series, find the most recent date before t with sales > 0, then compute current_date - last_sale_date in days.

Meaning: Number of non-zero sales days in past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(7).apply(lambda x: sum(x>0)).

Meaning: Number of non-zero sales days in past 28 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(28).apply(lambda x: sum(x>0)).

Meaning: Share of zero-sales days in past 28 days
How to build this feature?: Compute 1 - nonzero_count_28 / 28, or rolling mean of (sales==0) after shift(1).

Meaning: Whether item sold at least once in past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Create 1 if nonzero_count_7 > 0 else 0.

Meaning: Whether item sold at least once in past 28 days
How to build this feature?: Create 1 if nonzero_count_28 > 0 else 0.

Meaning: Mean sales over past 14 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(14).mean().

Meaning: Mean sales over past 56 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(56).mean().

Meaning: Median sales over past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(7).median().

Meaning: Median sales over past 28 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(28).median().

Meaning: Maximum sales over past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(7).max().

Meaning: Maximum sales over past 28 days
How to build this feature?: Group by store_id + item_id. Use sales.shift(1).rolling(28).max().

Meaning: Average sales from same weekday over last 4 weeks
How to build this feature?: For each series, use lag_7, lag_14, lag_21, lag_28 and take their mean.

Meaning: Average sales from same weekday over last 8 weeks
How to build this feature?: For each series, use lag_7,14,21,28,35,42,49,56 and take their mean.

Meaning: Median sales from same weekday over last 8 weeks
How to build this feature?: For each series, compute median of lag_7,14,21,28,35,42,49,56.

Meaning: Current price vs historical average price
How to build this feature?: For each store_id + item_id, divide current sell_price by expanding mean of past sell_price values shifted by 1.
Already in current model?: Mentioned in Meeting, not in current notebook

Meaning: Current price vs historical max price
How to build this feature?: For each store_id + item_id, divide current sell_price by expanding max of past sell_price values shifted by 1.

Meaning: How far below historical max price current price is
How to build this feature?: Compute 1 - sell_price / historical_max_price using past prices only.

Meaning: Weeks since last price change
How to build this feature?: Within each store_id + item_id weekly price series, count weeks since sell_price changed from prior week.

Meaning: Whether current price dropped vs last week
How to build this feature?: Create 1 if sell_price < lagged sell_price else 0.

Meaning: Average dept sales in same store over past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Aggregate daily sales by store_id + dept_id + date, compute shifted rolling 7-day mean, then merge back.

Meaning: Average category sales in same store over past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Aggregate daily sales by store_id + cat_id + date, compute shifted rolling 7-day mean, then merge back.
Already in current model?: Notebook only has coarse cat/store features

Meaning: Average category sales in same state over past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Aggregate daily sales by state_id + cat_id + date, compute shifted rolling 7-day mean, then merge back.

Meaning: Average same item sales across all stores over past 7 days
How to build this feature?: Aggregate daily sales by item_id + date, compute shifted rolling 7-day mean, then merge back.

Meaning: Whether tomorrow is an event day
How to build this feature?: Using calendar by date, map event indicator of date+1 back to current row.

Meaning: Whether two days later is an event day
How to build this feature?: Using calendar by date, map event indicator of date+2 back to current row.

Meaning: Whether yesterday was an event day
How to build this feature?: Using calendar by date, map event indicator of date-1 back to current row.

Meaning: SNAP flag relevant to the row’s state only
How to build this feature?: If state_id is CA use snap_CA, if TX use snap_TX, if WI use snap_WI.
Already in current model?: Current notebook keeps all SNAP flags; this is cleaner
